title: Don’t Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus! The Musical!
tagline: Whatever you do, don’t let the Pigeon star in his own musical production!
description: See Mo Willems' Don’t Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus! brought to life as a 75-minute musical at CM Performing Arts Center in Oakdale. The Pigeon’s attempts to drive the bus play out with playful songs by Deborah Wicks La Puma and lively staging—great for kids ages 3 and up. Kids can laugh, sing along, and watch a favorite picture book become a live story; tickets start at $20.
description_source: Don’t Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus! The Musical! is a delightful musical adaptation of Mo Willems' beloved picture book. It features music by Deborah Wicks La Puma and guarantees fun for the entire family.

title: Earth Month Intro to Composting
tagline: Learn the basics of composting with the Urban Park Rangers
description: Join the Urban Park Rangers at Blue Heron Nature Center to learn how composting works and how to keep a bin in a city home. Kids ages 5–12 will get hands-on making a bottle compost bin (bring a plastic bottle or use one provided). It’s a short, practical workshop where children can try sorting scraps and see how waste turns into soil. Free event.
description_source: Join the Urban Park Rangers and learn the basics of composting. You will learn how to start and maintain a compost bin as well as the different ways you can keep one in an urban setting. Then put those skills to the test and make your own bottle compost bin. Bring your own plastic bottle or they will be provided.
subway: Staten Island Railway to Oakwood Heights

title: Museum Adventure Activity: Sea Survival Challenge
tagline: Do you have what it takes to survive the open ocean?
description: Follow clues through the Whaling Museum to solve puzzles about shipwrecks, castaways, and ocean science. Kids ages 5–16 will practice observation and problem-solving as they hunt for clues and complete challenges, then make a Navigator’s Necklace with a working compass. $5 for members; $10 plus museum admission for others.
description_source: Do you have what it takes to survive the open ocean? Embark on an adventure through the museum to collect clues and crack puzzles as you follow the paths of shipwrecked sailors, clever castaways, and daring ocean scientists. Cap off your quest by creating a Navigator’s Necklace complete with a working compass!

title: Zoo Explorers: Bugs and Butterflies
tagline: Discover the wonders of insects at the Bronx Zoo!
description: At the Bronx Zoo, kids ages 8–10 get an up-close encounter with a small insect ambassador. They’ll try simple care tasks, see how keepers provide enrichment, and learn the skills used to look after butterflies and other bugs. Fee: $60 non-member / $48 member.
description_source: Have you ever wondered how zookeepers take care of the butterflies and bugs at the Bronx Zoo? title: Long Island Tulip Festival at Waterdrinker
tagline: Get Ready for a Bloomin' Good Time!
description: Walk among over 2.5 million tulips at Waterdrinker Family Farm & Garden in Manorville, with two farms to explore and U‑pick stems for $1 each. The festival also offers mini golf, barnyard animals, a Little Amsterdam walk‑thru, a greenhouse and a farm brewery. Great for families—kids can meet animals, pick flowers and run in the fields. Admission: $20 weekdays, $25 weekends.
description_source: Visit us for a vibrant celebration of spring as we bring the beauty of Holland right here to Long Island. This year, we planted over 2.5 million Tulips at our 2 farms and can't wait to show you all!
price_summary: Weekdays: $20 per person; Weekends: $25 per person

title: Walk For Resilience 5K Run/Walk
tagline: A community-centered mental health event
description: This is a community 5K run/walk hosted by Carmel’s Depression & Anxiety Wellness Foundation to raise awareness and promote mental wellness. Families (ages 5–99) can walk, jog, or run at their own pace while kids get to move, hear a DJ, grab light snacks, and pose for free finish-line photos. Free to join at Prospect Park with timing, bibs, water, and live results.
description_source: The Walk for Resilience 5K Run/Walk is hosted by Carmel’s Depression & Anxiety Wellness Foundation (CDAWF). This inclusive 5K event brings together walkers, runners, families, and supporters to raise awareness for depression and anxiety while promoting movement, connection, and healing. Designed for all fitness levels, participants may walk, jog, or run at their own pace. The event emphasizes togetherness rather than competition, creating a supportive environment for mental health conversations.

title: Katy’s Courage 5K Run/Walk
tagline: Run/Walk for Local Scholarships and Cancer Research
description: A community 5K run/walk in Sag Harbor that raises money for local scholarships and cancer research through Katy’s Courage. Families and kids ages 5–100 can join — participants get a t-shirt, DJ music, hot chocolate, donuts and bagels, plus age-category awards for top finishers. Registration runs $25–$33.80.
description_source: Join us for the Katy’s Courage 5K Run/Walk in Sag Harbor, NY on April 18, 2026. The event starts at 8:30 AM, rain or shine. Proceeds from the event will go to Katy's Courage, supporting local scholarships and cancer research. Participants can enjoy amenities like t-shirts, music, and refreshments, along with awards for top finishers in various categories. Register now to secure your spot!

title: International Public Policy Forum (IPPF) Finals Weekend
tagline: Compete for the grand prize and Brewer Cup
description: Watch the finals of the International Public Policy Forum, the culmination of a six‑month international debate series where high school teams argue global policy ideas. Finals weekend at NYU’s King Juan Carlos Center includes an educational symposium, live debates on a G20 education tax motion, and the $10,000 prize and Brewer Cup. Free with RSVP; best for students 15–18 who want to see policy debate and hear different viewpoints up close.
description_source: New York, March 18, 2026 — The Brewer Foundation is pleased to announce the 'Elite 8' teams in the 25th anniversary International Public Policy Forum (IPPF). The advancing teams earn an all-expenses-paid trip to New York City to compete in the IPPF Finals, where the IPPF World Champions will be awarded a $10,000 grand prize and the coveted Brewer Cup. Founded in 2001, the IPPF is jointly administered by the Brewer Foundation and New York University and is the world’s only international debate competition for high school students with a global policy focus.

title: Fourteenth Annual Katy’s Courage 5k
tagline: Join us for a community run to support local scholarships!
description: Join the fourteenth annual Katy’s Courage 5K in Sag Harbor to help fund local scholarships. It’s a family-friendly community run where kids (ages 5–12) can try a real 5K, run with neighbors, and earn a finisher medal. Register $25 in advance or $30 day-of; check-in 7–8:15 a.m., start 8:30 a.m.
description_source: Katy’s Courage (P.O. Box 3251, Sag Harbor, NY 11963) is hosting the fourteenth annual Katy’s Courage 5K on Saturday, April 18, rain or shine. Net proceeds from this year’s race will support Katy’s Courage, a 501(c)(3) organization, with a portion of funds allocated to local scholarships. Participants may register online in advance at https://katyscourage.org. On race day, registration and check-ins will take place from 7 to 8:15 a.m. and the run will begin promptly at 8:30 a.m.

description: The Lion King at the Minskoff Theatre is a stage adaptation of the Disney film with large-scale puppets, elaborate costumes and the original songs. They’ll experience live music, visual storytelling, and moments about friendship, courage and responsibility. All guests need tickets (from $111).

title: Dorothy’s Adventures In Oz
tagline: An unforgettable trip down the Yellow Brick Road
description: Dorothy’s Adventures in Oz brings The Wizard of Oz to life at Theatre Three in Port Jefferson. Kids (recommended 5–12) follow Dorothy, the Scarecrow, Tinman and Lion down the Yellow Brick Road with a new original score and family-friendly performances. Children can sing along, see colorful costumes, and learn about friendship and courage. Tickets $15.
description_source: Join us for an unforgettable trip down the Yellow Brick Road with Dorothy’s Adventures in Oz. Dorothy Gale is whisked away by a tornado to that magical land that lies just Over the Rainbow. Follow Dorothy and her friends – the Scarecrow, the Tinman, and the Lion - as they encounter challenges and celebrate friendship. Our brand-new take on this classic tale features an original score, memorable characters, and fun for the entire family.

title: South Street Seaport Museum Lenape Games
tagline: Discover Lenape culture through traditional games and interactive experiences.
description: Play traditional Lenape games like Selahtinalìtin (a straw-scattering game) and try hands-on activities led by instructors at the South Street Seaport Museum. Kids (recommended ages 5–12) will practice simple skills, take turns, and hear stories about Lenape traditions as they play. Open to all ages and free to attend.
description_source: Join us for an exciting day exploring the Lenape culture through games that were played by the Lenape people. This interactive experience is designed for families and children of all ages, and it provides a fun and educational way to learn about history. Participants will engage with hands-on activities that reflect the traditional games of Native Americans, ensuring a memorable and educational experience for everyone.

title: Hicks Nurseries’ 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show
tagline: Game of Gardens
description: At Hicks Nurseries' 36th Flower & Garden Show families can wander eight board-game–themed gardens planted with over 5,000 blooms. Kids (best for ages 5–12) can join a free scavenger hunt and weekend "Plant a Pea" activity to pot and take home a seedling. LEGO Make‑and‑Take workshops for ages 5+ are April 4 ($20). Admission and parking are free.
description_source: Hicks Nurseries invites visitors to step into a world where beloved games meet breathtaking blooms at its 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show, Game of Gardens, running March 5–29, daily from 8am–6pm. The event features eight uniquely designed gardens showcasing over 5,000 plants. title: Cumbe Center for African Dance
tagline: An interactive experience celebrating African rhythms
description: At the Staten Island Children’s Museum, Cumbe Center leads an interactive African dance and music session for kids ages 5–12 (free with admission). Children try traditional instruments, learn lively steps, and hear the stories behind the rhythms. It’s hands-on movement that builds rhythm, teamwork, and cultural curiosity.
description_source: Feel the joy, energy, and heartbeat of rhythms rooted in African culture! In this interactive experience, kids won’t just listen – they’ll move and groove. Young participants will explore traditional African instruments, discover the stories behind the beats, and learn vibrant dances that celebrate community, creativity, and connection. Get ready to clap, stomp, and dance your way through an unforgettable cultural adventure!

title: Sea Shanty Sessions
tagline: Join maritime music enthusiasts for a sing-along of authentic sea shanties in a historic setting.
description: Sing along to traditional maritime songs at the Noble Maritime Collection, led by the Folk Music Society of New York. Free, family-friendly sessions (third Sunday, 2–4 PM) honor sailor-song collector William Main Doerflinger. Kids can learn call-and-response shanties, try simple rhythms, and hear short stories about life at sea.
description_source: Led by the Folk Music Society of New York, this program offers a great opportunity to experience authentic, time-honored maritime songs in a historic setting. Sea Shanty Sessions are dedicated to William Main Doerflinger (1909-2000), the music scholar who collected traditional sailor’s songs from the residents of Sailor’s Snug Harbor. The sessions are free and family-friendly, and you are encouraged to sing along if the mood strikes you!

title: The Planter and the Plant – A Workshop Performance
tagline: A journey exploring the relationship between a planter and a plant.
description: A 30-minute theatre workshop-performance at QED Astoria for kids aged 3–8 and their families, free to attend. Children follow a planter and a seed as the plant grows, watching their relationship shift from curiosity to care and farewell. Short pre- and post-show activities let kids try ideas and share feedback while the piece is in development.
description_source: The Planter plants. Food, drinks, anything. One day, a seed grows into a plant. What follows is a journey exploring their evolving relationship: from getting to know the plant, to witnessing its unique growth, and finally, its departure. This is a theatre piece for 3–8 year olds and their families. This is a work-in-progress performance of a new theatre piece. We’re still in the exploration stage, and workshopping is the best way to gather audience feedback and discover what’s working, and what isn’t.

title: Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography
tagline: The first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture from the Museum’s film stills archive since 1993
description: Face Value at MoMA is a free exhibition of over 200 Hollywood studio portraits from 1921–1996, featuring stars like Louis Armstrong, Bette Davis and Diana Ross. It shows how studio photographers shaped magazine and press images before the digital age. Families can have kids spot familiar faces and compare lighting, poses and expressions to see how portrait photos are made.
description_source: The Museum of Modern Art announces 'Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography', the first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture to be drawn from the Museum’s film stills archive since 1993. On view in the Titus and Morita Galleries, the exhibition will offer a revisionist look at the Department of Film’s photographic archive, examining the evolution of editorial practice before the digital age, AI technology, and social media reshaped the experience of celebrity.

This event is perfect for young budding scientists and reptile enthusiasts alike!

title: ASL Slam at Lincoln Center
tagline: Join the celebration of American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community
description: ASL Slam is a live showcase of American Sign Language poetry and storytelling at Lincoln Center’s David Rubenstein Atrium, part of the Big Umbrella Festival. Teens (13–18) will watch traditional, contemporary and experimental signed pieces, meet performers from the Deaf/signing community, and see how ASL uses rhythm, space and facial expression to tell stories. Free entry.
description_source: Having just celebrated their 20th anniversary in 2025, ASL Slam joins the Big Umbrella Festival at the David Rubenstein Atrium this April. The company’s mission is to preserve, nurture, showcase, and promote American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community through performance by offering a stage for community members to share and create traditional, contemporary, and experimental sign language literature. ASL Slam provides a safe space for the Sign Language community to play with their language and art.

title: MainStage Concert: The Places That Inspire Us
tagline: Music inspired by real-world settings
description: At Our Saviour’s Atonement, WHCO presents Anna Clyne’s Restless Oceans alongside Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915, with soprano Ariadne Greif. The program explores music that paints sea turbulence and childhood memories, so expect vivid, narrative-driven pieces. Good for listeners who enjoy emotionally rich classical music. Tickets $16.25–$21.50.
description_source: In The Places That Inspire Us, we explore music inspired by real-world settings and the emotional landscapes they evoke. The program begins with Anna Clyne’s Restless Oceans, a thrilling contemporary work for chamber orchestra that channels the turbulence and strength of the sea. This work draws inspiration and its title from A Woman Speaks – a poem by Audre Lorde. Next on the program, WHCO is joined by Ariadne Greif for Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915, a nostalgic meditation on childhood...

description: Forever K-Pop is a live tribute concert at St. George Theatre performing hits from BTS, BLACKPINK, NewJeans and more. title: Patterns at Lincoln Center
tagline: A multi-sensory dance performance by AXIS Dance Company for all ages, part of the Big Umbrella Festival
description: Patterns is a multi-sensory dance performance by AXIS Dance Company at Alice Tully Hall, part of the Big Umbrella Festival. D/deaf, neurodiverse, disabled and non-disabled dancers share live work and short interactive moments where kids can move along. Best for ages 5–18; pay-what-you-can (suggested $5).
description_source: Led by Artistic Director Nadia Adame and Executive Director Danae Rees, AXIS Dance Company is one of the nation’s most acclaimed ensembles of disabled, non-disabled, D/deaf, and neurodiverse performers. AXIS was founded in 1987 and is based in Berkeley, California, where the company creates world-class productions that challenge perceptions and redefine dance and disability. Alongside its artistic programming, the company provides unparalleled integrated dance education and outreach programs.

title: New York City Tartan Day Parade
tagline: Celebrate Scottish-American heritage
description: The Tartan Day Parade marches up Sixth Avenue from West 45th to West 55th at 2:00 PM, running about two hours. Expect thousands of participants — bagpipers, Highland dancers and clan groups — showing Scottish music, dance and dress. Great for families: kids can hear the pipes, see dancing and learn a bit about Scottish-American heritage. Free to attend and march.

title: Family Concert: Music of Mozart
tagline: Explore the music of Mozart with your family!
description: Spend an afternoon at The Morgan with Young Concert Artists playing music by Mozart. Kids ages 5+ can listen, sing, and move along, then try hands-on artmaking and explore the Mozart exhibition. Tickets include museum admission; $30 adult/$10 child ($20 adult for members).
description_source: Visit the Morgan for an afternoon concert for our youngest visitors! This family-friendly performance with Young Concert Artists invites children to listen, sing, and move as they explore the music of Mozart. Pre- and post-concert activities include artmaking and a gallery exploration in the exhibition, Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart: Treasures from the Mozarteum Foundation of Salzburg. Recommended for families with children ages 5 and up. Families with children of all ages and abilities are welcome.

price_summary: $30 per adult; $10 per accompanying child. $20 per adult, $10 per each accompanying child for Morgan Members. Each ticket includes admission to the Morgan the day of the concert.

title: Two if by Sea: Lantern Workshop
tagline: Join us for a fun and illuminating craft workshop!
description: Join the Whaling Museum in Cold Spring Harbor on the anniversary of Paul Revere’s ride for a hands-on lantern workshop. Kids (ages 5–12) will hear how sailors used lanterns and how signals guided events during the Revolution, then make and decorate a decoupaged jar lantern. Suitable for young crafters; $8 members / $12 participants, and each child takes their lantern home.
description_source: Join us on the anniversary of Paul Revere's famous midnight ride for a fun and illuminating craft workshop! Discover how lanterns have been used to keep sailors safe at sea and to send secret signals during the American Revolution. Create a festive, decoupaged lantern jar to light up your home!

title: Peter and The Wolf
tagline: A family-friendly ballet adventure
description: See Prokofiev’s Peter and the Wolf performed as a full ballet with live music, costumes and sets at Ballet Long Island in Ronkonkoma. Recommended for ages 3–10 (ideal 5–10), it introduces classical instruments and storytelling through dance so kids can follow the characters and hear which instruments represent each one. Tickets $25 adults, $15 children.

title: Antarctica! Crew Wanted
tagline: Crew wanted for a bold sensory expedition
description: Antarctica! Crew Wanted is an immersive sensory theatre for neurodivergent kids ages 6–10, inspired by Shackleton’s 1915 expedition. Through music, movement and hands-on sensory moments, children step into icy scenes, try teamwork tasks and explore survival ideas. At Samuel Rehearsal Studio; suggested $25.
description_source: Created for young people ages 6 and up who are neurodivergent, Antarctica! Crew Wanted is a theatrical experience that guides participants through an immersive, sensory-filled world, depicting the icy land of Antarctica. An Irish production created by sensory artists Phillida Eves and Amélie Bal, this theatrical performance with music and movement is inspired by Ernest Shackleton’s epic Antarctic expedition of 1915, when his ship Endurance was trapped in the ice and ultimately sank.

title: Downtown Brooklyn Car-Free Earth Day 2026
tagline: Join an afternoon of Earth-friendly and car-free activities
description: Spend an afternoon car-free at Albee Square with free family activities for kids 5–14. Children can learn to ride confidently with Bike New York, join soccer sessions with Brooklyn FC, try hand-sewing with Black Girls Sew and make art from reused materials; there's also e-waste drop-off and DJ music. Free.
description_source: Join an afternoon of Earth-friendly and car-free activities including music, fashion, bike lessons, and more. Albee Square West will be closed to traffic, so bring your bikes or roller skates, and enjoy activities and music at Albee Square. Here’s what’s happening: On Albee Square West 12:00 PM – 3:00 PM In partnership with Elian Electronics Recycling, you are invited to dispose of your e-waste in a free, convenient, and ethical way. 1:00 PM – 3:00 PM Kick-off spring with action-packed soccer fun sponsored by Brooklyn FC, Brooklyn’s professional soccer team. Bike lessons by Bike New York for kids aged 5-14. Hand sewing with Black Girls Sew. Art projects from reused materials.

title: ARF’s Kitten Shower
tagline: The CUTEST baby shower of the year… is for kittens!
description: Bring kids to ARF’s Kitten Shower at the ARF Adoption Center in East Hampton to learn about caring for newborn kittens. Families (best for ages 5–12) can watch bottle‑feeding demos, try kitten‑themed crafts, and help gather supplies for foster kittens. It’s free and a hands‑on way for kids to see how volunteers care for and prepare kittens for adoption.
description_source: Kitten season is almost here, and ARF Hamptons is getting ready to welcome dozens of tiny, wiggly, purr-filled kittens. Every spring, vulnerable little ones arrive who need round-the-clock care—many are orphaned and rely on bottle feeding, warmth, and loving foster homes until they’re ready for adoption. You’re invited to celebrate at ARF’s annual Kitten Shower! Come help us gather supplies, learn how to care for bottle babies, and meet a few of our tiniest arrivals.

title: ASL Baby Slam at Lincoln Center
tagline: A family-friendly showcase promoting playful language acquisition through sign language.
description: ASL Baby Slam at the David Rubenstein Atrium is a free, family-friendly performance of American Sign Language as part of the Big Umbrella Festival. Kids can watch signed stories and join interactive language play to try simple signs, rhythm, and gestures. It's suited for babies, toddlers and caregivers who want a welcoming way to explore ASL and meet the Deaf community.
description_source: Having just celebrated their 20th anniversary in 2025, ASL Slam joins the Big Umbrella Festival at the David Rubenstein Atrium this April. The company’s mission is to preserve, nurture, showcase, and promote American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community through performance by offering a stage for community members to share and create traditional, contemporary, and experimental sign language literature. title: Turtle Time
tagline: Explore the fascinating world of turtles!
description: Turtle Time is a 30-minute interactive session at Prospect Park Zoo where kids meet a live turtle up close. Children ages 5–10 and their caregivers will learn how turtles, tortoises, and terrapins differ, explore shell biology and survival adaptations, and hear a bit of their evolutionary story. Tickets are $10 ($8 members).
description_source: This fun, family-friendly program is all about turtles! In just 30 minutes, we’ll explore the fascinating differences between turtles, tortoises, and terrapins by meeting one of these amazing animals up close. Discover how these incredible reptiles survive in different habitats, what makes their shells so special, and why they’ve been around for millions of years. Perfect for curious kids and their caregivers, this engaging session will leave you turtally amazed!

title: Newsies JR.
tagline: A timeless story of rebellion, song, and spirit, based on the true 1899 newsboys' strike in New York City.
description: Disney’s Newsies JR. at The Secret Theatre is a 60-minute youth production of the Broadway musical about the 1899 newsboys’ strike. Packed with energetic song-and-dance numbers by Alan Menken and Jack Feldman, it follows Jack Kelly and the newsies as they organize against unfair price hikes. Great for kids 7–12 (recommended from 5), kids will enjoy lively performances and a simple lesson about standing up for fairness. Free admission.
description_source: Disney’s Newsies JR. is a 60-minute version of the 2012 Broadway musical, based on the 1992 film. Inspired by the rousing true story of newsboys in turn-of-the-century New York City, Newsies JR. features a Tony Award-winning score by Alan Menken and Jack Feldman and a book by Tony Award winner Harvey Fierstein. Celebrate 45 years of endless fun chomping Pac-Dots and Ghosts. It’s a Power Pellet party for fans of all ages!

title: Ensemble Spotlight Series
tagline: Easter afternoon pageant of symphonic sounds and vocal treasures
description: Spend Easter afternoon at Stern Auditorium listening to youth orchestras and choirs from the Carolina Youth Symphony, Spring Valley Academy Band, and the Crystal Children’s Ensemble. Kids ages 3–12 will hear lively symphonic and choral pieces by Rutter, Whitacre and others, and see peers perform on a big stage. Family-friendly pacing and tickets from $46.
description_source: MidAmerica Productions presents an Easter afternoon pageant of symphonic sounds and vocal treasures. The Ensemble Spotlight Series features the return of the Carolina Youth Symphony, conducted by Charles Daniel Baldwin; the debut of the Spring Valley Academy Band, led by Donald Huff; and the Crystal Children’s Ensemble, led by Karl Chang, performing choral works by Rutter, Whitacre, Chilcott, Cao, Chou, and more.

title: Easter Parade and Easter Bonnet Festival
tagline: Join the festive celebration of Easter in New York City!
description: The Easter Parade on Fifth Avenue is a free, family-friendly pageant of people showing off bonnets as they march north from 49th to 57th Street, centered near St. Patrick’s Cathedral (about 10am–4pm). It’s a long-running city tradition celebrated in song by Irving Berlin. Kids will love the colorful hats and costumes — bring a bonnet if you want to join in.
description_source: Each year on Easter, celebrants don festive finery and show off their very best bonnets along Fifth Avenue. Immortalized by Irving Berlin (with some help from Judy Garland and Fred Astaire), the pageant is a New York City tradition that stretches back to the 1870s. Starting at about 10am and continuing until 4pm, the parade marches north on Fifth Avenue, from 49th Street to 57th Street. The best place to watch is from the area around St. Patrick's Cathedral; better yet, bring your bonnet and join the parade.

title: Easter Bunny 5K Race Run/Walk
tagline: Join us for a fun family-friendly race!
description: Come to Eisenhower Park on April 5 for the Easter Bunny 5K—Kids Fun Run starts at 8:30 AM, 5K at 9:00 AM. title: New England Symphonic Ensemble plays Lauridsen, Mozart and Vivaldi
tagline: An Easter evening program of choral masterworks and contemporary sacred music
description: Spend an Easter evening at Carnegie Hall hearing choir and orchestra perform Mozart, Vivaldi, Lauridsen and a contemporary piece by Johnny Edwards. Kids (0–12) can listen for different voices and instruments, and hear how music from Baroque to modern changes mood and story. Tickets start at $35.
description_source: The New England Symphonic Ensemble presents an Easter evening program that spans early Classical and Baroque choral masterworks alongside contemporary sacred music. Under the baton of Terrance Cook-Green, the choir and orchestra perform Mozart’s Missa brevis in D Minor, K. 65, paired with Johnny Edwards’ modern sacred work Risen. title: MOCACREATE: Celebrate Qing Ming & Welcome Spring with Teaching Artist Yu Rong
tagline: A workshop celebrating spring with art
description: Bring kids (ages 5–10) to the Museum of Chinese in America for a hands-on Qing Ming workshop with Teaching Artist Yu Rong. Children will sculpt clay tea eggs, oranges, cookies and figures to build a picnic scene, then make paper flower bouquets to remember loved ones. All materials provided; free to attend.
description_source: It’s time for the Qing Ming festival, when families remember those who’ve passed on and welcome spring. Families gather to honor their ancestors and make ritual offerings of food and flowers. Mold clay people, tea eggs, drum sticks, oranges, almond cookies, and more to create your very own Qing Ming picnic scene. Then create a paper flower bouquet in memory of those you love.

title: NYSoM Easter Eggstravaganza 2026
tagline: Spring celebration with fun activities for kids!
description: Bring kids to NYSoM’s Easter Eggstravaganza at Sherman Creek Park on April 4, 2–5 PM. Free admission; attendees should follow mask and distancing guidance.

title: ASPIRE 10K
tagline: Join the 48th Annual ASPIRE 10K Run/Walk
description: The ASPIRE 10K is a community run/walk on April 4, 2026 at H.B. Mattlin Middle School in Plainview, raising funds for the Sunrise Association. Open to ages 5–99, participants get a race shirt, finisher medal, post-race refreshments, and age-group prizes. Kids can try a real race, enjoy the crowd, and feel proud crossing the finish line. Registration required; early bird starts at $44.15.
description_source: The ASPIRE 10K is a community-driven event taking place on April 4, 2026, at H.B. Mattlin Middle School in Plainview, NY. Participants can expect top-quality amenities including a race shirt, finisher medal, and post-race refreshments. There are also prizes awarded for various age groups. This event primarily benefits the Sunrise Association, which supports children with cancer and their siblings through summer camps and activities.

tagline: Join TiCK MiTT for a morning walk on the Upper East Side!
description: Join TiCK MiTT for a 90-minute morning dog walk on the Upper East Side, meeting at LB Coffee (454 E 84th St) and heading to Carl Schurz Park. Expect wagging dogs, free coffee and snacks, and practical, down-to-earth tips on tick prevention for city pet owners. Bring kids (0–10) to learn how to spot and avoid ticks while enjoying the walk and meeting dogs.

title: Urban Environmental Challenge Race
tagline: A fun trail race in the Bronx!

title: My Magical Chivita / Mi Chivita Mágica
tagline: An unforgettable journey across Colombia
description: Join Lucy as she searches for the chivita her Abuelo gave her, traveling through Bogotá, Cartagena, Cali and Medellín in a bilingual show at Teatro SEA (The Clemente). Kids ages 3–10 will hear songs, see puppets, and pick up Spanish words while exploring Colombian rhythms and stories. Tickets $20.
description_source: All aboard for adventure! Young Lucy has lost touch with her Colombian roots and misplaced the precious chivita (miniature bus) her beloved Abuelo Rafael gave her before she left Colombia. What begins as a search for a treasured keepsake becomes an unforgettable journey across Colombia—from the heights of Bogotá to the coastal beauty of Cartagena, from Cali to Medellín. Through music, puppetry, and bilingual storytelling, this heartwarming tale celebrates Colombian culture while exploring themes...

title: Light, Form and Shadow Workshop with Jim Gingerich
tagline: Discover the foundation of strong drawing and painting by mastering the essential relationship between light, form, and shadow.
description: At Southampton Arts Center, kids ages 5–12 will draw a lit sphere to learn how light shapes form and shadow. They'll start with a circle and an oval for the cast shadow, then use grayscale tones to make their drawings look three-dimensional. Admission $5; Southampton students free.
description_source: Discover the foundation of strong drawing and painting by mastering the essential relationship between light, form, and shadow. In this hands-on class, students will work from a simple sphere placed under a focused light source. As the light casts a clear shadow across the table, you’ll learn how to observe and translate what you see into a convincing, three-dimensional image. Begin by drawing a circle to establish the sphere, followed by an oval to map the cast shadow. From there, using grayscale values, students render the form.
price_summary: $5 general admission; free for Southampton students

title: Events for Youth & Families: Women’s History Month
tagline: Honoring the artistry, strength, and cultural legacy of women
description: At Brooklyn Public Library’s Central Library, a 60-minute dance program for Women’s History Month features three pieces drawn from Chinese and Asian dance traditions. Kids (0–12) will see movement shift from graceful elegance to quiet lyricism and then to bold, heroic scenes. It’s free, and children get a lively, visual way to learn about women’s roles across cultures and history.
description_source: In celebration of Women’s History Month at the Brooklyn Public Library, this special performance honors the artistry, strength, and cultural legacy of women through three contrasting works drawn from Chinese and Asian dance traditions. The program moves from radiant elegance to intimate lyricism and, finally, to bold heroism—offering audiences a vivid and accessible way to reflect on women’s contributions across cultures and throughout history.

title: Annual Spring Egg Hunt at Belmont Lake State Park
tagline: Join us for the 30th annual egg hunt!
description: Bring the kids to Belmont Lake State Park for the 30th annual Spring Egg Hunt, free and open to the public. Timed hunts run every 15 minutes from 12:15–2:45 for ages 4 & under, 5–6, and 7–9 (3-egg limit per child), plus food vendors, a petting zoo, pony rides, educational displays, photos with the Spring Bunny, and a spin-the-wheel. Kids will hunt for eggs, meet animals, and take home treats.
description_source: The New York State Office of Parks, Recreation and Historic Preservation is pleased to announce that the 30th annual Spring Egg Hunt at Belmont Lake State Park will be held Saturday, March 28, 2026, from 12:00pm-3:00pm. The egg hunts will take place on the open field and will begin at 12:15pm. Additional hunts will occur at 15 minute intervals with the last one at 2:45pm. The hunts will be divided into three age categories: ages 4 & under; ages 5 & 6 and ages 7-9. There is a 3 egg limit per child per hunt to ensure everyone goes home with a special treat.

title: Quill Making
tagline: Learn how to make your own ink and write with quills, as was done when the Declaration of Independence was drafted.
description: Spend a morning with Urban Park Rangers at Blue Heron Nature Center making ink from natural materials and learning to write with quills. Children ages 5–12 will mix ink, dip a feather pen, and practice letters with hands-on instruction and all materials provided. It’s free and great for kids who enjoy crafts and history.
description_source: Join the Urban Park Rangers in taking a step back in time and learn how to make your own ink and write with quills, which was the very same way the Declaration of Independence and other historical documents were written before modern writing utensils.

title: 3 on 3 Basketball Tournament
tagline: Annual basketball tournament for grades 5–12
description: Join the Town of Southampton Youth Bureau's annual 3-on-3 basketball tournament for grades 5–12 (ages 10–18) at Southampton Youth Services on March 27. Sign-in and warm-ups are 6:00–6:45 PM; games run 7:00–10:00 PM; teams of 3–6 players, $45 per team or $15 per player — pre-register by March 23. Kids will play short, fast games, practice quick offense/defense, and compete for prizes in middle- and high-school brackets.
description_source: The Town of Southampton Youth Bureau will host its annual 3-on-3 Basketball Tournament on Friday, March 27 from 7:00 PM to 10:00 PM at Southampton Youth Services (SYS), 1370A Majors Path, Southampton. Sign-in and warm-up drills will take place from 6:00 PM to 6:45 PM. The tournament is open to youth in grades 5–12, with prizes awarded to winning teams in both middle school and high school brackets. Space is limited. Team pre-registration of $45 is required by Monday, March 23, 2026. Each team member must submit a completed registration form.

title: Easter Egg Hunt in the Trees
tagline: A thrilling egg hunt up in the trees!
description: At The Adventure Park at Long Island, kids ages 5–8 climb between platforms to hunt for Easter eggs tucked in the trees. They’ll practice basic climbing, balance and observation while searching and earn a sweet treat at the end. The hunt area is open, but each child needs a $57 climbing ticket. Great for active kids who like a little challenge.
description_source: Join the Park for an Easter Egg Hunt in the Trees, where the Easter Bunny has taken things to the next level—literally. He’s ditching the bushes and hiding eggs way up high! Your mission? Spot the eggs with your eyes as you climb and score a sweet treat at the end. Just remember, the hunt is free, but you’ll need a climbing ticket—because the Easter Bunny went all out hiding these eggs in the trees!

title: 50 Years of Apple Computers: The Kevin Lenane Collection
tagline: Explore the evolution of personal computing
description: See the most complete U.S. collection of Apple computers at the Long Island Museum, assembled and restored by Long Island collector Kevin Lenane. Kids (6+) can get up close to a rare 1976 Apple‑1 and early Lisa and Macintosh artifacts and learn how home computing began and changed over time. Admission from $15; children under six free.
description_source: Explore the most complete collection of Apple computers in the United States, assembled and restored by Long Island resident Kevin Lenane. His collection celebrates the technological and design innovations of Apple that brought personal computing devices into the homes—and now the hands—of all Americans. The exhibition features a rare 1976 Apple-1 computer, marking the 50th anniversary of its release, alongside artifacts from Apple’s groundbreaking Lisa and Mac development projects of the 1980s.
price_summary: Adults $15; Seniors (62+) $10; Students (6-17, with ID) $10; Persons with disability $5; Children under six Free; Active/Retired Military Free; LIM Members Free

title: The Museum of Modern Art Presents A View from the Vaults: The 1980’s
short_title: A View from the Vaults: The 1980’s
tagline: Explore the dynamic decade of filmmaking.
description: MoMA's "A View from the Vaults: The 1980s" screens films from its collection, from Die Hard to Béla Tarr's Almanac of Fall and Kurosawa's Ran. All titles are shown in archival 16mm and 35mm prints, so you’ll see the theatrical look and sound of the decade. Free entry at MoMA; recommended for viewers 16 and up who enjoy film history and varied styles.
description_source: This installment of our A View from the Vaults series focuses on works in MoMA’s collection from the 1980s, a dynamic decade marked by blockbuster films, a buzzing theater-going culture, and new modes of discovery. This collection showcase includes films such as Béla Tarr’s Almanac of Fall, John McTiernan’s Die Hard, and Akira Kurosawa’s Ran, among others, representing a vibrant decade of filmmaking.

title: The Museum of Modern Art Presents Frida and Diego: The Last Dream
tagline: Celebrating the legacy of Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera
description: At MoMA, "Frida and Diego: The Last Dream" brings together works by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera alongside the Metropolitan Opera’s new production, showing how their art connects to music and theater. The installation’s bold colors, murals, and personal stories make it easy for kids to spot themes like identity and family. Free for ages 16 and under; adults $17–$30.
description_source: The Museum of Modern Art announces an art exhibition, _Frida and Diego: The Last Dream._ _Frida and Diego: The Last Dream_ celebrates Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera—two of Mexico’s most beloved icons of 20th-century art—in a first-of-it-kind collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera. Presented in conjunction with the Met’s new production of _El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego_ (May 14–June 5, 2026), composed by Gabriela Lena Frank with a libretto by Nilo Cruz, the exhibition features artworks by Kahlo and Rivera.
price_summary: Included with admission: $17–$30; free for ages 16 and younger
reviews: ['A powerful visual dialogue between Kahlo’s intimate self-portraits and Rivera’s monumental murals. The Met opera connection adds unexpected depth.', 'Well-curated and immersive. The Bausor installation transforms the gallery into a theatrical space that honors both artists’ legacies.', 'Perfect for teens and adults interested in 20th-century art. Younger children may find it static, but the scale and color hold attention.']

title: Checkmate at BFPL
tagline: Play, watch, and learn the game of chess!
description: Spend two weeks at Brookfield Place’s Winter Garden playing, watching, and learning chess for free, co-sponsored by the Battery Park City Authority. Daily open play and a tenant tournament run 10am–6pm, and a Saturday skills workshop (ages 5+) includes simultaneous play with a Grandmaster. Kids can try new tactics, get hands-on tips, and play others their level.
description_source: Brookfield Place (BFPL) is proud to present Checkmate at BFPL, a two-week inclusive event offering the opportunity to play, watch, and learn the game of chess for all. The free programming is co-sponsored by Battery Park City Authority (BPCA) and will take place in BFPL’s Winter Garden. During Checkmate at BFPL, there will be a variety of activities offered, including open play, a tournament for Brookfield Place office tenants, and a Saturday chess skills workshop open to all ages and levels with simultaneous play featuring a Grandmaster.

title: Photos With The Bunny at Queens Center
tagline: Schedule Your Photo Experience to Meet the Easter Bunny
description: Bring kids to Queens Center Mall in Flushing to meet the Easter Bunny and get a keepsake photo — admission is free. Kids (0–12) can sit with the bunny, pick up a Yankee Candle photo label, and try a 3‑month Crayola Scribble Scrubbie Pets app trial. Reservations cut wait times; sensory‑friendly sessions and Pet Night available.
description_source: Join us for a magical experience as you take photos with the Easter Bunny at Queens Center Mall! Enjoy exclusive perks when you book your visit in advance, including a free photo label from Yankee Candle and a 3-month trial of the Scribble Scrubbie Pets app from Crayola. Reservations are recommended to reduce wait times, but walk-ins are welcome! Special experiences like sensory-friendly Bunny photos and Pet Night are also available.

title: Junior Explorers: Kings of the Sea
tagline: Explore the underwater world of California Sea Lions!
description: At Queens Zoo in Corona, kids in grades K–5 (ages 5–11) get a hands-on look at California sea lions. They’ll learn how sea lions differ from seals, why they vocalize, and what adaptations help them swim and hunt. The session mixes up-close viewing with simple activities so children can explore and ask questions. Admission is $36 per person.
description_source: What’s the difference between a sea lion and a seal, and why are sea lions so loud? You’ll learn this and more as you explore the underwater world of our California Sea Lions. Dive in and discover the unique adaptations that make sea lions the kings of the water. K-5th grade.

title: Celebrate 45 Years of PAC-MAN at The Paley Museum
tagline: A retro-cool family day with arcade games, PAC-MAN meet-and-greets, and arts and crafts

tagline: A magical marionette performance
description: Puppetworks’ marionette version of Aladdin brings the story to life with a flying carpet, a vanishing castle and an otherworldly Djinni, all set to Aram Khachaturian’s Gayane music. tagline: Celebrating Trans Voices in Horror

All proceeds were donated to Advocates for Trans Equality, a nonprofit organization fighting for the rights of trans people in America. Readers include: Megan M. title: Peter and the Wolf (and Friends!)
tagline: An interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s musical tale
description: Bring kids to Greenwich House Music School for a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s Peter and the Wolf, where each character is represented by a different instrument. It's a short, kid-friendly show for ages 0–8 where children listen, laugh, and help guess who's who. After the performance, kids can try instruments at a petting zoo. Tickets free–$37.
description_source: Join us for Peter and the Wolf (and Friends!)—a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s beloved musical tale where each character comes to life through a different instrument. In this shorter, kid-friendly version, children will laugh, listen, and help guess who’s who as they meet Peter, his animal pals, and the not-so-scary wolf. But the fun doesn’t end there! Peter brings along a few surprise musical guests—other classical favorites designed to delight and engage the audience through interactive moments and an instrument petting zoo at the end.

title: Dead Father, Stepfather, Girl Father — A Comedy Show by A Chinese Queer Kid
tagline: A unique blend of comedy, drag, and political performance exploring queer identity and familial loss.
description: This solo show mixes stand-up, drag, burlesque and political theater as a Chinese queer performer digs into three decades of searching for father figures. Drawing on life in China, Taiwan and the U.S., it looks at grief, patriarchy and whether chosen family can replace biological ties. For ages 21+, tickets about $24–25 at Caveat.
description_source: It has been three decades since this only child from China began longing for approval and affection from multiple father figures—one deceased, one failed, and one who turns out to be more than just a father. After violence, grief, disappointment, and rebuilding, she/they begins to wonder: as a queer feminist, does one really need a father figure at all? After all, she has spent her entire life fighting patriarchy—personally and politically. Moving between China, the U.S., and in between, Taiwan,...

title: Ice Queen Performance
tagline: A frosty adventure in Central Park
description: See a 45-minute musical at Buttenwieser Hall about a brave girl crossing a wintry Central Park to rescue her friend from the Ice Queen. Original songs, clear storytelling and snowy visuals keep kids ages 2–12 engaged. Children follow the action, learn about teamwork and friendship, and sing along. Tickets start at $32.
description_source: A young girl sets out across Central Park in a frosty gale to rescue her best friend from the clutches of an evil Queen. Through snow and ice and a slew of adventures, what follows is a timeless story — a very NYC retelling of the tale that inspired Disney’s Frozen — about the changing seasons of friendship that will warm the hearts of young audiences and their families alike. This musical is 45 minutes long and best enjoyed by audience members ages 2+.

title: Sunday Funday at Bronx Children's Museum

title: Anish Kapoor: Mirror Works
tagline: Explore the reflective world of Anish Kapoor
description: At Lisson Gallery, Anish Kapoor’s mirror installations use polished stainless steel to bend light and reflections across large sculptures. Children (recommended 6–12) can watch themselves warp and shift in the surfaces, notice how space stretches, and compare different views up close. Free entry makes it an easy visit to spark questions about perception and light.
description_source: Anish Kapoor’s work transcends traditional boundaries, encouraging viewers to engage with the environment around them through reflective materials. The exhibition features large-scale installations and intimate sculptures that invite observers to reconsider their own presence and experience in the art world, using light, reflection, and form to challenge spatial perception.

title: Faith Ringgold: Artist, Storyteller, Activist
tagline: Exploring diversity through literacy and storytelling.
description: This exhibition at the Sugar Hill Children's Museum displays Faith Ringgold’s paintings, illustrated scenes from MLK Jr.’s Letter from Birmingham Jail, working drawings and children’s-book manuscripts. Kids ages 5–12 can explore storytelling and social justice through bold images and story-quilt ideas, seeing how an artist turns thoughts into books. Free admission makes it easy to bring the family.
description_source: Artist, writer, educator and activist Faith Ringgold was born in Harlem, not far from the Sugar Hill Museum. It was here that she had her first art studio, became an art teacher and began to raise her own family. Her love for the children of Harlem inspired Broadway Housing Communities to create the museum. title: YOAH
tagline: Cirquework & Cluster Arts Global from Fukuoka, Japan
description: YOAH is a wordless contemporary circus from Japan mixing acrobatics, dance, traditional drumming and electronica with striking projections. Kids ages 7–12 will watch juggling, glowing diabolos and daring chair towers as a young wanderer meets Shinto spirit figures. At the New Victory Theater, children will feel the rhythm, see new circus skills up close, and leave inspired; tickets start at $25.
description_source: Established in 2021, Cirquework is a Japanese circus company founded by young circus artists active in Japan and other nations. Combining acrobatics, dance, music, lighting and visual effects, they create unique and innovative contemporary circus experiences for audiences. In the moonlit glow of dawn, a young wanderer named YOAH descends from her aerial silks into a circle of Shinto spirits, made manifest as mysterious maestros of cirque! Hypnotic projections play in sync with the performers as they send juggling balls soaring, conjure confidence atop a tower of chairs and fling their glowing diabolos to the heavens in hopeful arcs of neon. Set to a dramatic medley of traditional Japanese drumming and thumping electronica, this wordless symphony of circus will dazzle.

title: Hicks Nurseries’ 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show
tagline: Step into a world where board games meet blooms.
description: Take the kids to Hicks Nurseries’ 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show, a free indoor event in Westbury running March 5–29. The greenhouse has eight game-themed gardens with over 5,000 plants, perfect for little explorers and photo ops. Children (0–12) can do a scavenger hunt, join LEGO Make-and-Take workshops, and try planting peas to learn basic gardening.
description_source: February 4, 2026 – Westbury, NY — Hicks Nurseries, Long Island’s premier garden center, invites visitors to step into a world where beloved games meet breathtaking blooms at its 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show, Game of Gardens, running March 5–29, daily from 8am–6pm. The much-anticipated free indoor event transforms one of the Hicks Nurseries 20,000-square-foot greenhouses into a playful show inspired by the joy, creativity, and nostalgia of classic games. Guests will explore eight uniquely designed gardens and over 5,000 plants.
price_summary: Free admission; paid workshops available separately.

title: Walk-In! Workshop: Kinetic Sculptures

title: Aladdin & the Wonderful Lamp
tagline: A Tale from 1,001 Arabian Nights adapted for Marionettes
description: At Puppetworks in Brooklyn, marionettes bring Aladdin and the Arabian Nights to life in a playful puppet show. Best for ages 5–10 (age 3+ welcome), kids watch characters move, follow the story, and get a close-up lesson in imagination, problem-solving, and courage. Tickets are $12; $11 child.
description_source: Join us on Saturdays at 12:30 pm or 2:30 pm through April 5. Experience the enchantment of Aladdin's story brought to life with marionettes.
reviews: ['My 6-year-old loved the puppets — the genie was hilarious and the music kept her engaged the whole time.', 'Great for preschoolers. The theater is small and intimate, making it perfect for young kids who get overwhelmed in big venues.', 'Well-produced marionette show. Story was simplified just right for kids under 10. Left with a happy child and no meltdowns.']
